State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660          322-5901         322-6441              322-6444

December 7, 1983


Eduardo A. Rivera
City Attorney
P.O. Box 1587
Calexico, CA  92231

                                          Re:  Your Request for Advice, Our
                                               Advice No. A-83-253

Dear Mr: Rivera:

     Mr. Pat Hashem, a candidate for the City Council of the City
of Calexico, has requested that you seek our advice as to a
possible conflict of interest situation which might arise if the
candidate is successful in being elected to the Council in April
1984. The facts are as follows:

                                    FACTS

     1.   On August 16, 1982, the candidate entered into a
land lease agreement with the City of Calexico for a period
of ten (10) years.  The lease will terminate on August 16,
1992.  He is currently paying a monthly rental of $650.

     2.   The candidate has announced his intent to run for a
council seat in the April 1984 Municipal Elections.  If
elected in the April 1984 Municipal Elections, the
candidate's term would be from April 1984 to April 1988.

     3.   If the candidate is successful in the April 1984
Municipal Elections, a conflict of interest would be
created, pursuant to [Calexico's] Conflict of Interest Code,
requiring disclosure and disqualification of any action
pertaining to the candidate's lease.

                                    QUESTION

     You have requested the Commission's advice on the following
question:

     Apart from disclosure and disqualification from any
action, would any other liability be created under the
applicable Government Code Conflict of Interest sections?

Eduardo A. Rivera
December 7, 1983
Page 2


     In addition, in your first and then second letters you have
asked our advice regarding the possible implications under
Government Code Section 1090, et seq. Because we do not
administer or enforce that provision of the law,1/ we are
unable to advise you as to those questions. You may wish to
consult with the Attorney General on that point.

                              ANALYSIS

     The Political Reform Act provides that:

          No public official at any level of state or local
     government shall make, participate in making or in any
     way attempt to use his official position to influence a
     governmental decision in which he knows or has reason
     to know he has a financial interest.

                              Section 87100.

     A financial interest in a decision exists whenever there
will be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on:

          (b)  Any real property in which the public
     official has a direct or indirect interest worth more
     than one thousand dollars ($1,000).

     An "interest in real property" is defined in Section 82033
to include "any leasehold ... interest ... in real property
located in the jurisdiction...." Clearly, in this situation the
leasehold has a value in excess of $1,000. However, because the
leasehold has less than 10 years remaining, it will not be
reportable under the provisions of Section 82033 should the
candidate be elected and take office in 1984. However, the
leasehold interest could form the basis for possible
disqualification.2/


___________________
     1/  The Fair Political Practices Commission administers,
implements, enforces and interprets the Political Reform Act of
1974 (Government Code Sections 81000-91014). Government Code
Sections 83111-83116. All statutory references are to the
Government Code unless otherwise specified.

     2/  See the Commission's Overstreet Opinion, 6 FPPC
Opinions 12, No. 80-010, March 2, 1981, copy enclosed.

Eduardo A. Rivera
December 7, 1983
Page 3


     Disqualification of Mr. Pat Hashem would be required as to
any decision which would have a material financial effect on
his leasehold interest where that effect is distinguishable from
the decision's effect upon the public generally.3/  If
Mr. Hashem becomes a member of the City Council of Calexico and
a specific decision presents itself raising this issue, do not
hesitate to again seek our advice.

     If you have any questions regarding this letter, I may be
reached at 916/322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Robert E. Leidigh
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

REL:km
Enclosures

_______________________

     3/  This term is defined in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18702, copy enclosed.

     4/  This term is defined in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18703, copy enclosed.

NOV 3 IZ 15 FM 03

November 3, 1983

Fair Political Practices Commission
110 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

ATT: Advice Letter Section

Dear Sir or Madam:

I am the City Attorney for the City of Calexico. A
candidate for a council seat on the City Council for
the City of Calexico has requested of me an opinion
regarding a possible conflict of interest.

The facts constituting the possible conflict of
interest are as follows:

1. On August 16, 1982, the candidate entered
into a land lease agreement with the City of
Calexico for a period of ten (10) years. The
lease will terminate on August 16, 1992. He
is currently paying a monthly rental of $650.

2. The candidate has announced his intent to
run for a council seat in the April 1983
Municipal Elections. If elected in the April
1983 Municipal Elections, the candidate's
term would be from April 1984 to April 1988.

3. If the candidate is successful in the April
1983 Municipal Elections, a conflict of interest
would be created, pursuant to our Conflict of
Interest Code, requiring disclosure and dis-
qualification of any action pertaining to the
candidate's lease.

The opinion requested from the Commission is as follows:

1. Apart from disclosure and disqualification
from any action, would any other liability be
created under the applicable Government Code
Conflict of Interest sections?

430 Mary Avenue, P.O. Box 1587 · Calexico, California 92231 · Telephone 619 / 357-6801

Advice Letter Section
November 3, 1983
Page Two


     2.  Would Government Code Section 1090 require that
the candidate, if successful, relinquish his lease
with the City of Calexico?

Would you be kind enough to provide me with comments on the
above two (2) questions.

If you should have any further questions, please feel free to
contact this office.

     Atentamente,

EDUARDO A. RIVERA
Attorney at Law

EAR/mcm

Statements of Economic Interest
322.6444

November 10, 1983


Eduardo A. Rivera
Attorney at Law
P.O. Box 1587
Calexico, CA 92231

Re: A-83-253

Dear Mr. Rivera:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Robert E. Lefelich, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief, Legal Division

NOV 14 12 40 PM '83

November 9, 1983

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Advice Letter Section

Dear Sir or Madame:

I am the City Attorney for the City of Calexico. On
November 3, 1983, I requested an opinion from the
Fair Political Practices Commission Advice Section
regarding a conflict of interest in regards to a
candidate for a City of Calexico Council seat, a copy
is attached for your review. A further opinion is
requested as to the Government Code Section 1090 issue:

In the event that, in your opinion,
Government Code Section 1090 would indicate
that the candidate, if successful, should
relinquish his lease, would it be possible
for the candidate to have his lease operated
and/or administrated under some allowable
trust arrangement. We have enclosed a copy
of his lease agreement to aid you in this
determination.

If you should have any further questions, please feel
free to contact this office.

Atentamente

EDUARDO A. RIVERA
Attorney at Law

     3.  If the candidate is successful in the April

The opinion requested from the Commission is as follows:

     1.  Apart from disclosure and disqualification
from any action, would any other liability be
created under the applicable Government Code
Conflict of Interest sections?

2                                    PREAMBLE

3         It is intended by the Parties that LESSEE is to use said

4    premises for purposes of the storage of all types of motor vehicles

5    and motorized or non-motorized carriers, including, but not by way

6    of limitation, the storage of Greyhound and/or any other type of

7    buses, vans or trailers.  The property would be used for storage

8    purposes and for purposes relating to or incidental thereto such as

9    the cleaning, maintenance and repair of the aforementioned vehicles

10   and/or carriers.

11

12         IT IS HEREBY AGREED THAT:

13   I.   PARTIES:

14         The City of Calexico, a municipal corporation, hereinafter

15   referred to as LESSOR, and PAT HASHEM, hereinafter referred to as

16   LESSEE, do hereby enter  into this lease on August 16, 1982.

17   II.  DESCRIPTION:

18         LESSOR, for and in consideration of the terms, conditions and

19   covenants herein contained, does hereby lease, demise and let to

20   the LESSEE, and the LESSEE hereby leases and rents from LESSOR that

21   certain property described as:

22                    To be added and marked Exhibit "A"

23                    and incorporated by reference and
                      as fully set forth.

24   III.  TERM:

25         The term of this lease is to run for a period of One Hundred

26   Twenty (120) months, commencing August 16, 1982.

27   IV.  RENT:

28         The rental payments shall be for the sum of Six Hundred Five

29   Dollars ($605), and payable in advance on the sixteenth day of each

30   month commencing August 16, 1982.  Said rent is to be adjusted

31   annually by 10 percent over the term of the lease. The initial sum

32   paid shall include the first and last month's rent.

V. PURPOSE:

It is understood and agreed that LESSEE is to use said

premises for the purpose of providing storage for all types of

motor vehicles and motorized or non-motorized carriers, including,

but not by way of limitation, the storage of Greyhound and/or any

other type of buses, vans or trailers. The property would be used

for storage purposes relating to or incidental thereto such as the

cleaning, maintenance and repair of the aforementioned vehicles

and/or carriers.

VI. CONDITION - RIGHT OF ENTRY FOR INSPECTION:

The LESSOR does hereby retain the right to enter the premises

upon the giving of a reasonable notice and at a reasonable time to

inspect the facility as to its maintenance and repair.

VII. COMPLIANCE WITH CITY, STATE AND FEDERAL LAWS:

The LESSEE hereby agrees to comply with all City, State and

Federal ordinances and statutes that are applicable to this lease

agreement.

VIII. ASSIGNMENT AND SUBLETTING:

LESSEE shall not sublet the premises or any part thereof, nor

assign this lease, nor any rights therein, nor in the leasehold

premises without written consent of the LESSOR. Any subletting or

assignment without first obtaining said written consent shall be

void and shall be cause for the cancellation of this lease by the

LESSOR at its option.

IX. LIABILITY OF LESSOR:

LESSEE agrees to save and hold LESSOR harmless from any injury

to person or damage to property arising out of or in connection with

LESSEE'S occupancy of the premises covered by this lease. LESSEE

agrees to indemnify LESSOR for any and all damages that may result

to LESSOR from LESSEE'S use of the facility pursuant to this agree-

ment.

-2-

X.  FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE:

     LESSEE agrees to carry fire and casualty insurance on the

building and premises in an amount of replacement cost.

XI.  PUBLIC LIABILITY INSURANCE:

     LESSEE shall provide public liability insurance for personal

injury or property damage arising out of or in connection with

LESSEE'S occupancy of the premises in an amount of not less than

One Million Dollars ($1,000,000). The LESSEE shall provide the

LESSOR with a Certificate of Insurance naming the City of Calexico

as an additional insured covering such liability.

XII.  MAINTENANCE:

     LESSEE agrees to maintain the building, structures and improve-

ments during the term of this lease in as good a condition as that

which they are received.

XIII.  REPAIRS:

     All repairs to the interior and exterior of the building,

structures and grounds including all maintenance and servicing, and

all repairs to and replacement including supplies, shall be at

LESSEE'S expense.

XIV.  UTILITIES:

     LESSEE agrees to pay for all utilities and services furnished

to said premises.

XV.  DEFAULT:

     If LESSEE, after notice, fails to remedy any default (a) in

the payment of any sum due under this lease for fifteen (15) days

or (b) in the keeping of any other term, covenant or condition of

this lease with all reasonable dispatch, not exceeding fifteen (15)

days, then the LESSOR or its agents may enter upon the demised

premises, take possession thereof, and remove all persons therefrom

and at LESSOR'S option, it may either terminate this lease and all

the LESSEE'S rights herein, or it may rent said premises for the

-3-

X. FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE:

LESSEE agrees to carry fire and casualty insurance on the
building and premises in an amount of replacement cost.

XI. PUBLIC LIABILITY INSURANCE:

LESSEE shall provide public liability insurance for personal
injury or property damage arising out of or in connection with
LESSEE'S occupancy of the premises in an amount of not less than

LESSOR with a Certificate of Insurance naming the City of Calexico
as an additional insured covering such liability.

XII. MAINTENANCE:

LESSEE agrees to maintain the building, structures and improvements during the term of this lease in as good a condition as that
which they are received.

XIII. REPAIRS:

All repairs to the interior and exterior of the building,
structures and grounds including all maintenance and servicing, and
all repairs to and replacement including supplies, shall be at
LESSEE'S expense.

XIV. UTILITIES:

LESSEE agrees to pay for all utilities and services furnished
to said premises.

XV. DEFAULT:

If LESSEE, after notice, fails to remedy any default (a) in
the payment of any sum due under this lease for fifteen (15) days
or (b) in the keeping of any other term, covenant or condition of
this lease with all reasonable dispatch, not exceeding fifteen (15)
days, then LESSOR or its agents may enter upon the demised
premises, take possession thereof, and remove all persons therefrom
and at LESSOR'S option, it may either terminate this lease and all
the LESSEE'S rights herein, or it may rent said premises for the

1 account of the LESSEE, or it may follow any other remedy provided

2 by law. Any holding over beyond term prescribed herein shall be

3 construed as month-to-month tenancy.

4 XVI. ALTERATIONS:

5 All alterations, additions, or improvements made in and to

6 said premises by LESSEE, shall unless otherwise provided by written

7 agreement between the parties hereto, be the property of LESSOR at

8 the expiration of said lease.

9 XVII. ATTORNEY'S FEES:

10 Should LESSOR be compelled to institute any legal action to

11 enforce any of the terms, covenants or conditions hereof, then in

12 the event of the termination of said proceedings in the LESSOR'S

13 favor, the LESSEE hereby agrees to pay such sums as the court may

14 deem reasonable on account of the LESSOR'S attorney's fees.

15 XVIII. BANKRUPTCY OR INSOLVENCY:

16 The LESSEE covenants that if a petition in bankruptcy shall be

17 filed by or against the LESSEE or if the LESSEE shall be adjudged

18 bankrupt or insolvent by any court, or if a receiver or trustee in

19 bankruptcy or a receiver of any property of the LESSEE shall be

20 appointed in any suit or proceeding brought by or against the

21 LESSEE, then and in each and every such case, the term hereby

22 granted shall immediately cease, terminate and come to an end, and

23 the LESSOR may recover and resume possession of the demised premises

24 by any legal means.

25 XIX. POSSESSORY INTEREST:

26 LESSEE recognizes and understands that this lease may create

27 a possessory interest subject to property taxation and that the

28 LESSEE may be subject to the payment of property taxes levied on

29 such interest.

30 XX. BINDING UPON HEIRS:

31 The covenants and agreements contained in this lease shall

32 [illegible]

1 inure to the benefit of and be binding on the parties hereto, their
2 heirs, legal representatives, successors or assigns.
3 XXI. NOTICE:
4          Wherever in this lease it shall be required or permitted that
5 notice of demand be given or served by either party to this lease
6 to or on the other, such notice or demand shall be given or served
7 and shall not be deemed to have been duly given or served unless in
8 writing and forwarded by certified or registered mail, addressed as
9 follows:
10          LESSOR: City Hall, 408 Heber Avenue, Calexico, CA 92231
11          LESSEE: Patrick R. Hashem, 123 First Street, Calexico, CA 92231
12 Such addresses may be changed from time to time by either party by
13 serving notices as provided above.
14 XXII. AGREEMENTS - MODIFICATION:
15          The agreement constitutes the full agreement between the
16 parties and can only be modified in writing and signed by both
17 parties.
18 XXIII. RENT ADJUSTMENT:
19          The rental payments shall be adjusted annually by a 10 percent
20 increase over the term of the lease.
21 XXIV. TERMINATION BY LESSEE:
22          LESSOR may terminate lease upon giving one year's notice of
23 said intent. LESSOR will reimburse LESSEE for the remaining
24 undepreciated costs for any building or structure constructed by
25 LESSEE in case of termination of lease.
26 XXV. IMPROVEMENT AND USE RATIFICATION:
27          LESSEE hereby agrees to comply with Resolution No. 3237. Said
28 Resolution requires the LESSEE to receive prior written confirma-
29 tion for any improvements made to the property and/or change of use
30 of the property. A violation of this section of the lease shall be
31 cause for default by the LESSEE.
32

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate for city council with an existing land lease from the city would face additional liability under the Political Reform Act beyond disclosure and disqualification requirements if elected.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that while the leasehold interest would not be reportable under Section 82033 due to having less than 10 years remaining, it could form the basis for disqualification on any council decisions materially affecting the lease. The FPPC declined to address Government Code Section 1090 questions, directing the city attorney to consult the Attorney General.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document includes incoming request dated November 3, 1983, acknowledgment letter dated November 10, 1983, supplemental inquiry dated November 9, 1983, and FPPC response dated December 7, 1983. The lease agreement is included as supporting documentation. Minor date discrepancy in incoming request (April 1983 vs April 1984 elections) was corrected in FPPC response.

question: 
Apart from disclosure and disqualification from any 
action, would any other liability be created under the 
applicable Government Code Conflict of Interest sections? 


Eduardo A. Rivera 
December 7, 1983  
In addition, in your first and then second letters you have 
asked our advice regarding the possible implications under 
Government Code Section 1090, et~. Because we do not 
administer or enforce that provislon of the law,ll we are 
unable to advise you as to those questions. 
You may wish to 
consult with the Attorney General on that point.

CONCLUSION: Disqualification of Mr. Pat Hashem would be required as to any decision which would have a material financial effect on his leasehold interest where that effect is distinguishable from the decision's effect upon the public generally. If Mr. Hashem becomes a member of the City Council of Calexico and a specific decision presents itself raising this issue, do not hesitate to again seek our advice.

The facts are as follows: FACTS 1. On August 16, 1982, the candidate entered into a land lease agreement with the City of Calexico for a period of ten (10) years. The lease will terminate on August 16, 1992. He is currently paying a monthly rental of $650. 2. The candidate has announced his intent to run for a council seat in the April 1984 Municipal Elections. If elected in the April 1984 Municipal Elections, the candidate's term would be from April 1984 to April 1988. 3. If the candidate is successful in the April 1984 Municipal Elections, a conflict of interest would be created, pursuant to [Calexico's] Conflict of Interest Code, requiring disclosure and disqualification of any action pertaining to the candidate's lease. QUESTION You have requested the Commission's advice on the following question: Apart from disclosure and disqualification from any action, would any other liability be created under the applicable Government Code Conflict of Interest sections? Eduardo A. Rivera December 7, 1983 Page 2 In addition, in your first and then second letters you have asked our advice regarding the possible implications under Government Code Section 1090, et seq. Because we do not administer or enforce that provision of the law,1/ we are unable to advise you as to those questions. You may wish to consult with the Attorney General on that point. ANALYSIS The Political Reform Act provides that: No public official at any level of state or local government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest. Section 87100. A financial interest in a decision exists whenever there will be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on: (b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000). An "interest in real property" is defined in Section 82033 to include "any leasehold ... interest ... in real property located in the jurisdiction...." embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter regarding conflict of interest concerns for a Calexico city council candidate who holds a 10-year land lease from the city. The Commission advised that disqualification would be required for decisions affecting the leasehold but declined to address broader Government Code Section 1090 implications.
